title: Getting Started with dafrjulia
text: dafrjulia R interface DataAxesFormats.jl, Julia package provides uniform interface accessing multi-dimensional data arranged along named axes. generalizes AnnData-like functionality, supporting scalars, 1D vectors, 2D matrices organized along arbitrary named axes efficient memory management. dafrjulia requires working Julia installation (>= 1.10). Install Julia https://julialang.org/downloads/.

title: Getting Started with dafrjulia
text: setup_daf() call initializes Julia runtime ensures required Julia packages available. needs called per R session.
code: library(dafrjulia)  # Initialize Julia and install required Julia packages (first time only) setup_daf()

text: simplest way get started -memory Daf: can also use file-based HDF5-based storage:
code: daf <- memory_daf("my_data") # File-based storage daf_files <- files_daf("/path/to/directory", "w", name = "files_data")  # HDF5-based storage daf_h5 <- h5df("/path/to/file.h5", "w", name = "h5_data")

title: Getting Started with dafrjulia
text: Vectors store one value per entry along axis:
code: set_vector(daf, "cell", "batch", c("B1", "B1", "B2", "B2", "B2")) set_vector(daf, "gene", "is_marker", c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE))  # Retrieve a vector (returns a named vector) get_vector(daf, "cell", "batch")

title: Getting Started with dafrjulia
text: Matrices store values combination entries along two axes:
code: umis <- matrix(     c(         10, 20, 0, 5, 15,         3, 0, 8, 12, 1,         0, 7, 4, 0, 9     ),     nrow = 5, ncol = 3 ) set_matrix(daf, "cell", "gene", "UMIs", umis)  # Retrieve a matrix (returns a named matrix) get_matrix(daf, "cell", "gene", "UMIs")

title: Getting Started with dafrjulia
text: dafrjulia provides composable query system extracting transforming data. Queries built using pipe operators: [ operator provides shorthand get_query():
code: # Get a vector property get_query(daf, Axis("cell") |> LookupVector("batch"))  # Filter cells by a condition get_query(daf, Axis("gene") |> LookupVector("is_marker") |> IsEqual(TRUE))  # Matrix operations with reduction get_query(daf, Axis("cell") |> Axis("gene") |> LookupMatrix("UMIs") |> Sum()) daf[Axis("cell") |> LookupVector("batch")]

title: R Interface to the Julia DataAxesFormats Package
text: goal dafrjulia provide R bindings DataAxesFormats.jl Julia package, provides uniform generic interface accessing 1D 2D data arranged along set axes. much-needed generalization AnnData functionality. dafrjulia package enables R users access Daf data using familiar R interfaces leveraging powerful implementation provided Julia package. package follows functional interface similar Julia implementation, limitations due limitations JuliaCall (see ).

title: R Interface to the Julia DataAxesFormats Package
text: R package provides interface similar Julia package, functions mirror Julia API:
code: library(dafrjulia) # Setup connection to Julia setup_daf() # Create a DAF object in memory daf <- memory_daf("example")  # Add axes add_axis(daf, "obs", c("cell1", "cell2", "cell3")) add_axis(daf, "var", c("gene1", "gene2"))  # Add vector data set_vector(daf, "obs", "score", c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9))  # Add matrix data mat <- matrix(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6), nrow = 3, ncol = 2) set_matrix(daf, "obs", "var", "counts", mat)  # Access data get_vector(daf, "obs", "score") #> cell1 cell2 cell3  #>   0.1   0.5   0.9 get_matrix(daf, "obs", "var", "counts") #>       gene1 gene2 #> cell1     1     4 #> cell2     2     5 #> cell3     3     6  # Get a dataframe view get_dataframe(daf, "obs", c("score")) #>       score #> cell1   0.1 #> cell2   0.5 #> cell3   0.9

title: R Interface to the Julia DataAxesFormats Package
text: One powerful features dafrjulia query language. can use queries access transform data flexibly. package provides convenient [ operator concise query syntax: query syntax follows simple pattern: @ axis selects axis . property retrieves scalar : property retrieves vector property :: property retrieves matrix property % operation applies element-wise operation >> reduction applies reduction operation [ property comparison value ] filters property See Julia documentation details query language.
code: # Create a sample dataset daf <- memory_daf("query_examples") add_axis(daf, "cell", c("A", "B", "C")) add_axis(daf, "gene", c("X", "Y", "Z")) set_vector(daf, "cell", "age", c(1, 5, 10)) set_vector(daf, "cell", "type", c("T", "B", "NK")) set_scalar(daf, "version", "1.0") set_matrix(daf, "cell", "gene", "counts", matrix(1:9, nrow = 3, ncol = 3))  # Basic queries using the [ operator # Get scalar values daf[". version"] # Get a scalar #> [1] "1.0"  # Access axes and properties daf["@ ?"] # List all axes #> [1] "gene" "cell" daf["@ cell : ?"] # List properties of the "cell" axis #> [1] "type" "age"  # Access vector data daf["@ cell : age"] # Get the "age" vector for all cells #>  A  B  C  #>  1  5 10  # Access matrix data daf["@ cell @ gene :: counts"] # Get the full counts matrix #>   X Y Z #> A 1 4 7 #> B 2 5 8 #> C 3 6 9  # Filter data daf["@ cell [ age > 2 ] : type"] # Get types of cells with age > 2 #>    B    C  #>  "B" "NK"  # Transform data daf["@ cell : age % Abs"] # Get absolute values of ages #>  A  B  C  #>  1  5 10 daf["@ cell : age % Log base 2"] # Get log2 of ages #>        A        B        C  #> 0.000000 2.321928 3.321928  # Aggregations daf["@ cell : age >> Max"] # Get maximum age #> [1] 10 daf["@ cell : age >> Mean"] # Get mean age #> [1] 5.333333  # Complex operations can be built using the pipe operator or query functions # Get types of cells with age > 2 Axis("cell") |>     BeginMask("age") |>     IsGreater(2) |>     EndMask() |>     LookupVector("type") |>     get_query(daf) #>    B    C  #>  "B" "NK"

text: dafrjulia package uses jlview zero-copy transfer arrays Julia R. However, writing data R Julia (e.g., via set_vector set_matrix) still involves copying JuliaCall. means get_empty_dense_vector, get_empty_sparse_vector matrix equivalents provide -place filling benefit offer Julia.

title: R Interface to the Julia DataAxesFormats Package
text: dafrjulia package inherits key features DataAxesFormats.jl: Support -memory persistent data storage Thread-safe implementation using read/write locks Ability import export AnnData objects Support views adapters Chaining repositories efficient reuse data Flexible data model based axes, vectors, matrices, scalar data Explicit support grouped axes Simple query language common operations Control data layout support dense sparse matrices

text: Wraps Daf object provides AnnData-compatible accessors ($X, $obs, $var, $layers, $uns). read-facade: reads lazy go underlying Daf object demand, benefiting dafrjulia's R-side caching. facade maps AnnData concepts Daf data: $X - primary matrix (obs_axis x var_axis, named x_name) $obs - data frame observation (obs_axis) vectors $var - data frame variable (var_axis) vectors $layers - named list additional matrices (excluding $X) $uns - named list scalars $obs_names - character vector observation names $var_names - character vector variable names $n_obs - number observations $n_vars - number variables $shape - c(n_obs, n_vars)

title: dafrjulia: R Interface to the Julia 'DataAxesFormats' Package — dafrjulia-package
text: R interface 'DataAxesFormats.jl' https://github.com/tanaylab/DataAxesFormats.jl, 'Julia' https://julialang.org/ package providing uniform interface accessing multi-dimensional data arranged along arbitrary axes. package generalizes extends 'AnnData'-like functionality, supporting 1D 2D data, multiple data modalities, efficient memory management. 'dafrjulia' enables R users create, read, modify, analyze complex biological datasets single-cell data multiple modalities (RNA-seq, ATAC-seq, etc.). package implements efficient memory management strategies provides seamless interface R 'Julia' handling large-scale biological data.

title: dafrjulia 0.1.1
text: Tracks DataAxesFormats.jl 0.3.0. DataAxesFormats 0.3.0 requires Julia 1.12 changed empty-buffer builder protocol writers.jl, broke wrapper’s filled_empty_sparse_vector / filled_empty_sparse_matrix (hit MethodErrors new signatures). Fixed: get_empty_sparse_vector / get_empty_sparse_matrix capture cache_group builder now returns hold paired filled_* call. filled_empty_sparse_vector / filled_empty_sparse_matrix thread cache_group Julia finalizer release data write lock builder opened (old _b binding helpers non-Julia wrapper used rely removed 0.3.0). get_empty_dense_vector / get_empty_dense_matrix unwrap buffer new (buffer, cache_group) return tuple release write lock (dense buffers separate finalizer step). public R API (function names, arguments, return shapes) unchanged. Full test suite passes DataAxesFormats 0.3.0 Julia 1.12.

title: dafrjulia 0.1.0
text: First CRAN release. Renamed dafr — dafr name freed native-R reimplementation separate repository. package JuliaCall-based wrapper around DataAxesFormats.jl renamed accordingly.

title: dafrjulia 0.1.0
text: library(dafr) becomes library(dafrjulia). options(dafr.JULIA_HOME = ...) becomes options(dafrjulia.JULIA_HOME = ...); dafr.julia_environment dafr.* options. Public API (function names, arguments, return shapes) unchanged.

title: dafrjulia 0.1.0
text: as_anndata(daf) returns DafAnnData R6 object exposing $X, $obs, $var, $layers, $uns, $obs_names, $var_names, $n_obs, $n_vars, $shape. Read-live: accesses go underlying Daf demand, served R-side cache hits. obs_axis/var_axis auto-detect cell/metacell gene; x_name validated construction.

title: dafrjulia 0.1.0
text: get_vector, get_matrix, axis_vector now cache results R side, keyed Daf-level version counters modifications via set_vector/set_matrix/add_axis invalidate entries automatically. cache env stored directly Daf object, global registry leak. Two R-level copies Daf list share cache (reference semantics); two wrappers Julia object get separate caches. empty_cache(daf, clear = ..., keep = ...) now always purges R-side cache (regardless selectors) validates arguments match.arg.

title: dafrjulia 0.1.0
text: New CreatedOutput expectation type, matching DataAxesFormats.jl v0.2.0. Like GuaranteedOutput, CreatedOutput item expected produced computation warns value already present; unlike OptionalOutput, never warns.

title: dafrjulia 0.1.0
text: Consolidated from_julia_array 9 sequential Julia↔︎R bridge calls 4 (5–6 named arrays). helper re-entrant uses structured Tuple return (Main-level globals, string delimiters). _prepare_for_r eagerly materialises dense non-zero-copy types (Bool, String, etc.) Julia, saving collect() round-trip R side. Version counters now fetched strings, preventing UInt32 overflow converting R integers. axis_version_counter, vector_version_counter, matrix_version_counter return character strings; compare identical() ==. _prepare_for_r flattens type dispatch reduce cold-start JIT cost.

title: dafrjulia 0.1.0
text: DESCRIPTION: updated Title, Description, URL, BugReports. cran-comments.md documents Julia dependency handling, \dontrun{} examples, vignette eval-, JULIA_AVAILABLE test guard. Vignette chunks explicitly eval = FALSE, purl = FALSE R CMD check’s tangle step try initialise Julia. inst/WORDLIST updated spelling checks. Julia-dependent tests guarded skip_if(!JULIA_AVAILABLE).

title: dafrjulia 0.1.0
text: jl_R_to_julia_type (internal) now validates string inputs whitelist regex rejects anything isn’t plausible Julia type name. Prevents arbitrary Julia code execution via type-conversion path. Daf(jl_obj) rejects non-JuliaObject inputs clear error instead passing silently.

title: dafrjulia 0.1.0
text: Package name. options library() calls . get_vector / get_matrix return values zero-copy types now live views Julia memory (via jlview / jlview_sparse). Copy explicitly (.numeric(v), .matrix(m)) stability across subsequent Daf modifications needed.
